title: The Iran War Sent Shock Waves Through Asia That Are Likely to Spread
summary: The Asia-Pacific was hit hard and quick by the war in Iran and its energy bottlenecks. Scenes of crisis there indicate that problems are multiplying and spreading.

title: Vance Heads to New Talks With Iran. At Stake: Peace and His Own Standing.
summary: The vice president is again center stage, after abruptly leaving the first round of high-level Iranian peace talks without an agreement.

title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Is at a Standstill Again
summary: Few vessels are crossing after Iran reversed course on reopening the vital waterway and ships came under attack.

title: U.S. Attacks and Seizes Cargo Ship Near Iran, and a First Step Toward Tariff Refunds
summary: Plus, Lego crime.

title: Trump Administration to Begin Refunding $166 Billion in Tariffs
summary: The government will debut a system to repay importers two months after the Supreme Court struck down tariffs at the heart of the president’s trade policy.

title: Why Your Paycheck Feels Smaller
summary: Ben Casselman, our chief economics correspondent, explains why wages are not keeping up with inflation and what that means for American workers and the economy.

title: Why Gas Prices Go Up Fast and Take So Long to Fall
summary: Fuel station owners take some of the hit for consumers when oil prices surge. On the way down, they try to get their money back.

title: Energy Secretary Says Gas Prices May Stay Above $3 Until 2027
summary: Secretary of Energy Chris Wright’s acknowledgment in a TV interview undercut President Trump’s earlier claim that price increases would be “short-term.”

title: Epstein Wanted Connections at Harvard. Files Show Many Faculty Members Were Happy to Help.
summary: New documents reveal what professors did to help Jeffrey Epstein get inside Harvard’s gates.

title: Starmer to Address UK Lawmakers on Mandelson Vetting
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer will address British lawmakers on Monday after it emerged that Peter Mandelson, his onetime ambassador to the United States, was rejected for top security clearances.

title: Ukraine, Short on Troops, Is Turning to Robots to Help Its War Efforts
summary: Ukraine is using unmanned ground vehicles armed with bombs, guns or rockets to carry out attacks and keep its soldiers out of harm’s way.

title: Why a Democratic Senate, Once Unthinkable, Is a Real Possibility
summary: Helped by a favorable national environment and strong candidate recruitment, Democrats are tied or ahead in four Republican-held seats, polls show.

title: Democrats Eye a Broader Battlefield to Capture Congress in November
summary: Weighed down by President Trump’s approval ratings, some Republican incumbents are struggling to raise money while Democrats look for targets like a Tennessee seat south of Nashville.

title: Big Names Wait in the Wings as Virginians Decide Their House Maps
summary: With Virginians voting Tuesday to accept or reject redistricting, candidates from both parties await the voters’ judgment to decide whether — or where — to run for Congress.

title: Tsunami Warnings Downgraded in Japan After Strong Offshore Earthquake
summary: The 7.7-magnitude undersea quake occurred off Iwate Prefecture, on the northeastern coast of Japan’s main island, Honshu.

title: The President Is Coming to the White House Correspondent’s Dinner
summary: A president who relishes attacking the news media is set to break his boycott of an event celebrating the news media. What could go wrong?

title: From Pulpit to Pews, Trump and Pope Are on the Minds of Catholics
summary: At churches with connections to Pope Leo and the Trump administration, pastors and parishioners speak out on the quarrel between the president and the pontiff.

title: U.S. Military Strikes a Boat in the Caribbean, Killing 3
summary: The latest attack raised the death toll to at least 180 in the campaign by the United States against people it accuses of smuggling drugs at sea.

title: A Leaner Saudi Arabia Turns From Grandiose Plans to Pragmatism
summary: A decade after Mohammed bin Salman unveiled his “Vision 2030” program to transform the country’s economy, the kingdom is facing financial strains and reassessing its trajectory.

title: Influencers Are Spinning Nicotine as a ‘Natural’ Health Hack
summary: The influencers, many of them aligned with the Make America Healthy Again Movement, say the medical establishment has unfairly demonized the compound.

title: Javier Milei Tamed Argentina’s Inflation. Now He Wants to Reshape Its Values.
summary: Argentina’s right-wing president has tamed the country’s runaway inflation. Now he wants to transform its values.

title: Haunted by ‘Dark Thoughts,’ Louisiana Father Kills 8 Children
summary: Seven of the eight children killed were the shooter’s own. Relatives said the gunman, who died in a confrontation with police, was struggling with mental health problems and stressed about his relationship with his wife.

title: C.E.O.s Are the Heads of Companies. Should They Also Be the Face?
summary: Most chief executives are not recognizable to their customers. But when they step into the limelight, the rewards — and the risks — can be great.

title: The Unlikely Recovery of America’s China-Shocked Towns
summary: The jobs are coming back, despite President Trump’s tariffs and harsh immigration enforcement.

title: Live Nation. Amazon. Americans Are Standing Up to Corporate Lawbreaking.
summary: Lina Khan and Doha Mekki on three court rulings that illustrate the power juries and local officials have to enforce antitrust laws.

title: Northeast Swings From 90 Degrees to Freezing Cold in Just a Few Days
summary: Central Park and other spots around the region set record-high temperatures last week. Freeze watches were in place on Monday. What’s going on?

title: New York New Jersey Stadium? Why MetLife Is Changing Its Name for the World Cup
summary: This summer, FIFA is putting its stamp on the venue for the World Cup.

title: Michigan Gas Clerk Helps Save Kidnapped Teen Girl Who Mouthed ‘Help’
summary: A fellow student who had witnessed the kidnapping called the police, and other students helped track the girl to a gas station.

title: A Humanoid Robot Races to a Record Half-Marathon Finish
summary: The android won a race featuring robots and humans on Sunday in Beijing, achieving a technological milestone while finishing faster than any person in history.
